warnings: fukui_charge_conservation_f+: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
fukui_charge_conservation_f-: sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Method-provenance summary:
Property: Condensed Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, dual descriptor) for benzaldehyde; most electrophilic site (atom 3, C) f⁺ = 0.39938968; most nucleophilic site (atom 1, O) f⁻ = 0.23839906 (units: electrons).
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (exact four-center integrals, no density fitting) in PySCF.
Geometry provenance: single-point calculations on the fixed Cartesian coordinates supplied in /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/benzaldehyde.xyz (no further optimisation; reported as DFT//input-geometry).
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet parent state (charge 0, multiplicity 1); cation (+1, doublet) and anion (–1, doublet) states generated automatically for the N±1 calculations.
Software/backend: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (engine subcommand “fukui”) using PySCF (version as bundled with chemkit).
Convergence & integrity: all SCF calculations converged; integrity.status = "ok", trustworthy = true. No separate warnings array was returned; two integrity checks of type “warning” report exact charge conservation (∑f⁺ = 1.0000, ∑f⁻ = 1.0000), reproduced verbatim below.

Live log: the calculation ran with live output to a *.out* file in the current working directory (path echoed at launch by chemkit; available for inspection or *tail -f*).

Verbatim warnings from the result JSON:
• fukui_charge_conservation_f+: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• fukui_charge_conservation_f-: sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

Headline result (most electrophilic condensed Fukui index): 0.39938968 electrons (atom 3, carbon).
